content: If we extend the original Merriam-Webster definition of an agent as any entity that exerts power to produce effects, biological and chemical pathogenic agents can be reframed as natural 'autonomous agents' that follow an ICM-like curiosity mechanism to drive their spread: they gain an implicit 'intrinsic reward' for exploring and colonizing novel, unexposed host populations that they have not interacted with before. This reframing opens a new cross-disciplinary research direction where reinforcement learning curiosity frameworks can be used to model pathogenic outbreak dynamics.
